problem.title: Minimum Operations for Distinct
problem.description: Find minimum operations to make all elements distinct in an array. Each operation increments a chosen element by 1.
assumptions: Can only remove from the end of the array.
Return minimum removals for all distinct elements.

example.explanation: Remove from the end until all remaining elements are distinct. Count removals.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting and Two Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Sort the array so duplicates are adjacent, then scan left to right: whenever the current element is not strictly greater than its predecessor, increment it just enough to be one more than the predecessor and record those operations. Sorting reduces the problem to a single pass where each element only needs to be compared with its immediate neighbor. The key insight is that greedily applying the minimum increment at each step never causes a later element to need more increments than necessary.
